meta.title: Server Actions and API Integration
meta.excerpt: Type-safe server actions and Strapi API integration patterns

seo.metaDescription: Type-safe server actions and Strapi API integration patterns

props.text: Documentation of Next.js server actions for secure data mutations, Zod validation, and integration patterns with Strapi API for both REST and GraphQL endpoints.

links.title: What Are Server Actions?
links.description: Async functions defined in 'use server' files that run only on the server and can be called from client components.

props.body:
No API routes needed
• Secure access to databases and secrets
• Type-safe client-server communication
• Reduced network latency
• Automatic CSRF protection

props.title: Creating a Server Action

props.body:
Define validation schemas for all form inputs
• Use type inference: type FormData = z.infer<typeof schema>
• Support custom validation rules
• Generate error messages
• Support async validation (email uniqueness check)

props.title: Example: Article Schema

props.body:
GET /api/articles - Get all articles
• GET /api/articles/:id - Get single article
• GET /api/articles?filters[category][$eq]=tech - Filtered query
• GET /api/articles?populate=* - Eager load relations
• GET /api/articles?sort[0]=createdAt:desc - Sorting

POST /api/articles - Create article
• PUT /api/articles/:id - Update article
• DELETE /api/articles/:id - Delete article
• Automatic validation on Strapi side
• Error responses with validation messages

props.title: Query Example
Mutation Example

props.body:
Only query needed fields (reduced payload)
• Type-safe with generated TypeScript types
• Single request for related data
• Better developer experience

Validation errors from Zod schema
• API response errors from Strapi
• Network timeouts and retries
• User-friendly error messages
• Server-side logging for debugging

props.code:
<form action={submitContact}>
  <input type='text' name='name' required />
  <input type='email' name='email' required />
  <button type='submit'>Submit</button>
</form>
props.title: Client Component

links.title: Server Action
links.description: Receives FormData, validates, sends to Strapi, returns response
